title: Mapped Field Selection Not Persisted in Slide-Out Panel
story: As a data migration specialist, I want previously mapped fields to remain visible when I reopen the field mapping slide-out, so that I can review and modify my mappings without losing context.
description:
When a user selects a field and maps it, then navigates away and returns to the same field, the previously mapped value was no longer visible in the slide-out panel. Additionally, attempting to map the same field from a different source field threw an error indicating it was already mapped, but without showing where.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Open a field mapping slide-out and select a target field to map
2. Navigate to the next field using the arrow controls
3. Return to the previously mapped field
4. The mapped value is no longer displayed

### Expected Behavior
When returning to a previously mapped field, the slide-out panel should display the existing mapping. The checkbox div showing the mapped data type should persist or be restored based on the selected source object.

### Root Cause
The checkbox div state was being cached incorrectly. When navigating between fields, the mapping state was not being properly restored from the underlying data.
acceptance_criteria: Previously mapped field values are visible when reopening the slide-out panel
Navigating between fields via next/previous arrows preserves mapping state
Duplicate mapping errors include information about the existing mapping location
Checkbox div is properly reset when the source object changes
Checkbox div is properly restored when returning to a previously mapped field with the same source object
ux_changes: Field mapping slide-out panel now correctly displays previously mapped values when a user navigates back to a mapped field. Checkbox div state is properly managed when navigating between fields.
middleware_changes: Updated the state management logic for field mapping to properly cache and restore checkbox div content per source object when navigating between fields.
implementation_notes: Two approaches were discussed: (1) clear checkbox div on navigation and require re-selection, or (2) persist checkbox div per source object across navigation. The final implementation persists the mapping state, restoring the checkbox div when returning to a previously mapped field with the same source object, and clearing it when the source object changes.
comment_summary: Extended back-and-forth between developer and reporter to clarify the exact bug behavior. Two solution approaches were discussed — clearing state on navigation vs. persisting per source object. Video walkthroughs were shared to demonstrate the issue. The persist approach was implemented.
